module.name: quantity
module.description: An inline physical quantity — value, optional ± uncertainty, and a unit (with ^ superscripts). E.g. 9.81 ± 0.02 m/s^2.
module.aiSpec:
An INLINE physical quantity: a value, an optional ± uncertainty, and a unit. Use it in running text where a measurement appears. This is an inline module — do NOT put math delimiters here (inline output is not re-typeset); write plain text. Parameters: `value` (text) — the number, e.g. 9.81 or 2.998e8. `unc` (text, optional) — ± uncertainty. `unit` (text, optional) — e.g. m/s^2, kg, °C. In the unit, `^n` becomes a superscript. `color` (color, default inherit) — color of the value. `unitcolor` (color, default muted) — color of the unit and uncertainty. `sci` (boolean, default false) — render exponent notation like 2.998e8 as 2.998×10^8. `bold` (boolean, default false) — bold the value.
The speed of light is <!-- @quantity value: 2.998e8, unit: m/s, sci: true -->. g = <!-- @quantity value: 9.81, unc: 0.02, unit: m/s^2 -->.

param.name: color
param.label: Value color
param.desc: Color of the value; blank = inherit text color.

param.name: unitcolor
param.label: Unit color
param.desc: Color of the unit and uncertainty; blank = muted.

snippet.label: Quantity (value ± unit)
snippet.description: Inline measurement with optional uncertainty and unit.
